items.title: At least 15 killed in southern Lebanon
items.description: The US embassy in Beirut said Lebanon is at a critical crossroads and called for a meeting between Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, despite a fragile situation that many Lebanese would find hard to describe as a true ceasefire. items.title: Opening Hormuz 'not a humanitarian gesture': Essential to keeping global food system operating
items.description: Amid geopolitical volatility and economic fragility, François Picard is pleased to welcome John W.H. Denton, Secretary General of the International Chamber of Commerce. Mr. Denton is warning that "the Strait of Hormuz is about much more than oil and gas": We are drifting toward a food security crisis of global proportions. While headlines fixate on oil and conflict, Denton insists that “the sharpest issue at the moment is actually the deterioration of access to fertilizer”, a development he links directly to “cataclysmic risk” for global food systems.

items.title: UK raises national terror threat level following antisemitic incidents
items.description: The UK has elevated its official terror threat level from substantial to severe, signaling that an attack is considered likely within the next six months. The move follows a series of antisemitic incidents, including the recent stabbing of two Jewish men in north London; the suspect was charged with attempted murder earlier today. In response, the government has labeled antisemitism an emergency and pledged increased security for Jewish communities. Meanwhile, Prime Minister Keir Starmer faced heckling on Thursday during a visit to the area where the attack occurred, as Peter O’Brien reports.

items.title: Germany: Stranded whale 'Timmy' being transported towards ocean in special barge

items.title: Venice Biennale jury resigns against participation of Russia and Israel
items.description: Geopolitical tensions have once again spilled into the world of contemporary art, as the entire international jury of the Venice Biennale resigned on Thursday, just nine days before the prestigious event was set to open. The move follows controversy surrounding Russia’s participation and a decision to bar prizes for countries accused of crimes against humanity, including Russia and Israel. As a result, the announcement of winners, typically held at the opening, has been postponed until the fair’s closing in November, as Morgan Ayre reports.

items.title: 'Military force unable to solve Mali crisis': Is political solution only 'viable path forward'?
items.description: As Mali's junta mourns the death of Defence Minister General Sadio Camara, with thousands coming out to pay tribute, FRANCE 24's François Picard welcomes Paul Melly, Consulting Fellow for the Africa Programme at Chatham House and expert on the Sahel region. He offers a sobering assessment of Mali’s deepening crisis, framing current events within both historical precedent and a radically altered geopolitical landscape. While echoes of the 2012 Mali crisis are unmistakable, "are we in the same situation as in 2012 when the French military had to swoop in to save Mali's capital from being overrun by insurgents?" asks François Picard.

items.title: The President and the Pope
items.description: Trump was paying the pontiff a compliment by taking him seriously. Instead of treating Leo’s remarks as the maunderings of an unworldly holy man who should be praised for his idealism and then ignored, as so many regularly do, he thought the Pope’s allusions were worth responding to. Leer más

items.title: Ugandan authorities arrest dozens of foreigners in possible human trafficking case
items.description: As Uganda presses ahead with its crackdown on illegal migration, authorities have arrested dozens of foreigners since Monday. The latest operation targeted a group of people living in the north of the country, and another group living in a closed compound.

items.title: UK police charge man with attempted murder for stabbing two Jewish Londoners
items.description: A 45-year-old British national was charged Friday over a stabbing attack on two Jewish men in north London. Both victims remain in stable condition.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Russian attack damages port infrastructure in Ukraine's Odesa region, governor says
items.description: Two high-rise apartment buildings ‌were damaged ​in the attack, which ⁠wrecked apartments and ‌caused fires, Mr. Kiper wrote on the Telegram messaging ‌app

items.title: News24 | Mosque mass killer Brenton Tarrant loses appeal to overturn New Zealand conviction
items.description: White supremacist killer Brenton Tarrant lost an appeal seeking to overturn his conviction and sentence for shooting dead 51 people at two New Zealand mosques in 2019, court documents showed.

items.title: United States confirms its neutrality on Malvinas after reported Pentagon memo
items.description: Washington’s “position on islands remains one of neutrality,” says US State Department; UK Foreign Secretary reiterates “Falkland Islands are British.” Leer más

items.title: Press freedom at lowest level in 25 years: RSF
items.description: "The average score for all countries and territories worldwide has never been so low," it said

items.title: Kosovo to hold snap elections in June
items.description: Kosovo will soon hold its third parliamentary election in a year and a half. Prime Minister Albin Kurti was able to form a government in February, but now there's a political deadlock over who shoud be elected president.

items.title: In Mauritania, Malian refugees hope to return home
items.description: Some 300,000 Malians have sought refuge in Mauritania's Hodh Chargui region since a security crisis began in 2012, fuelled by violence from jihadists, local criminal gangs and pro-independence groups

items.title: Libya, Algeria, Tunisia sign agreement to share Sahara aquifer water
items.description: The mechanism is also intended to prevent pollution and overexploitation of the water, with management and monitoring to be carried out jointly by the three countries.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Problems of success?
items.description: The transformation sought by President Javier Milei in Argentina runs into a delicate balance between the upward thrust of the new being created and the downward pull of the old being destroyed Leer más

items.title: Iran war: Mojtaba Khamenei says there will be 'change' in Strait of Hormuz 'management'
items.description: Iran's supreme leader issued a statement implying that Tehran wanted to keep its nuclear program and perhaps impose tolls in the Strait of Hormuz. Crude prices remain volatile. DW has more.

items.title: In five charts - How UAE's exit could affect Opec's influence over the oil price
items.description: The BBC takes a look in charts at what the UAE's departure could mean for the oil cartel and more widely.

items.title: How a controversial gas pipeline deal could further stall Bosnia's EU accession
items.description: A US backed gas pipeline could help Bosnia cut reliance on Russian energy. But Brussels warns that a deal with a company linked to Trump allies could put the country's EU hopes at risk.

items.title: News24 | Former FBI boss James Comey appears in court on ‘baseless’ Trump threat charge
items.description: Former FBI director James Comey surrendered to authorities in Virginia after being charged with threatening US President Donald Trump’s life with a social media post.

items.title: Maradona sisters, lawyer face trial over handling of star's brand
items.description: Late star’s lawyer and two of Maradona’s sisters – Rita and Claudia – will stand trial for the “fraudulent administration” of his brand and trademark. Leer más

items.title: DR Congo president orders audit of cobalt and copper export revenues
items.description: Felix Tshisekedi said poor oversight and capital flight were some of the issues preventing the country from fully benefiting from record sales.

items.title: Trump says Iran can play at World Cup in US after FIFA confirmation
items.description: FIFA chief Gianni Infantino has said Iran will play their World Cup matches, leading to agreement from US President.

items.title: Sabastian Sawe's parents overjoyed by London Marathon world record
items.description: Emily and Simon watched their son become the first athlete to run a sub-two-hour marathon from their home in Kenya.

items.title: Republic of Congo president holds talks with Russia's Vladimir Putin
items.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin held talks with Denis Sassou Nguesso, President of the Republic of Republic of Congo, at the Kremlin in Moscow on Wednesday.

items.title: 20,000 civilian sailors are stuck in the gulf that opens to the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Thousands of seafarers remain stuck as the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues. The United Nations urges for a plan to facilitate their release.

items.title: Man charged with attempted murder after stabbings of Jewish men in London
items.description: A 45-year-old man was charged with attempted murder in the stabbings of two Jewish men in London, the latest in a string of attacks that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.

items.title: Trump says US studying troop cuts in Germany, as spat with Merz intensifies
items.description: His remarks come after the German chancellor criticised the US approach to the war in Iran.

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: King Charles commemorates 9/11 victims in New York visit
items.description: The royal couple laid a bouquet at the September 11 Memorial and met with victim's families.

items.title: News24 | US Supreme Court voids Louisiana voting map amid national redistricting fight
items.description: The United States Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana’s electoral map was unconstitutionally drawn to create two black-majority districts.

items.title: Mirroring Gaza, Israel is destroying towns and villages in southern Lebanon
items.description: In southern Lebanon, towns near the border with Israel have been largely destroyed by Israeli demolitions and strikes. Israel says it has been attacking Hezbollah infrastructure, but civilian infrastructure has also been significantly affected.

items.title: Beyond oil: Niger Delta attracts new investments for economic growth and development, By Ehi Braimah
items.description: If the summit delivers even a fraction of its promise, the creeks that were once a major security flashpoint, may yet sparkle with a new possibility of attracting investors from around the world for the Niger Delta people and Nigeria. items.title: Fidelity Bank’s independent non-executive director to retire
items.description: The bank extolled Mrs Bammeke’s contributions to the growth and development of the bank during her tenure on the Board, noting she was outstanding in the various board committees. She assumed the role at the bank in 2021. The post Fidelity Bank’s independent non-executive director to retire appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: U.K. calls antisemitism an emergency after arson and stabbing attacks on Jewish people
items.description: The British government pledged to increase security for Jewish communities after a string of arson attacks and a double stabbing. But members of the community lashed out at the government.

items.title: Iran vows to protect its nuclear capabilities, signals ‘new rules’ for Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Iran will secure Persian Gulf and implement new rules to manage Strait of Hormuz, says Mojtaba Khamenei, calls U.S. presence the “most important factor of insecurity” in the region

items.title: Preserving the art of public speaking
items.description: English-Speaking Scholastic Association of the River Plate marks its centenary in style with final of national competition. Leer más

items.title: New Banksy sculpture appears to show politician blinded by his own flag
items.description: Banksy has unveiled a new sculpture of a man stepping off a stone base with his face obscured by a flag.

items.title: FIFA to review World Cup ticketing after backlash over 2026 prices
items.description: FIFA said Thursday it will review its ticketing strategy for the 2030 World Cup after a backlash over soaring prices for the 2026 tournament in North America, as officials defended costs as a reflection of demand while acknowledging fan anger.

items.title: News24 | ANALYSIS | Cape rerouting exposes SA’s maritime blind spots
items.description: More ships are rounding the Cape, but ports, policy and strategy are not yet built to turn the surge into lasting gain, write Daniela Marggraff and Timothy Walker in ISS Today.

items.title: Arsenal vs Fulham: Premier League – teams, start, lineups, title race
items.description: Arsenal will move six points clear of EPL title rivals Man City, who will have two games in hand, if they beat Fulham.
